name: MOQ Inventory Balance Calculator
description: Industrial-grade tool to calculate optimal inventory balance considering Minimum Order Quantities (MOQ), demand variability, and cost trade-offs. Aligns with Lean, Six Sigma, and WERC DC metrics.

inputs.label: Annual Demand (units)
inputs.businessContext: Total forecasted demand for the SKU over one year. Used to calculate order frequency and cycle stock.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total forecasted demand for the SKU over one year. Used to calculate order frequency and cycle stock.

inputs.label: Ordering Cost per Order ($)
inputs.businessContext: Fixed cost per purchase order (setup, administrative, freight). Influences EOQ and total logistics cost.

businessContext_i18n.en: Fixed cost per purchase order (setup, administrative, freight). Influences EOQ and total logistics cost.

inputs.label: Holding Cost per Unit per Year ($)
inputs.businessContext: Cost to hold one unit in inventory for one year (storage, insurance, obsolescence). Key for inventory carrying cost.

label_i18n.en: Holding Cost per Unit per Year ($)

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost to hold one unit in inventory for one year (storage, insurance, obsolescence). Key for inventory carrying cost.

inputs.label: Unit Cost ($)
inputs.businessContext: Purchase cost per unit from supplier. Used in total cost calculations and MOQ financial impact.

businessContext_i18n.en: Purchase cost per unit from supplier. Used in total cost calculations and MOQ financial impact.

inputs.label: Minimum Order Quantity (units)
inputs.businessContext: Supplier-imposed minimum batch size. If EOQ < MOQ, order quantity must be adjusted upward, increasing holding cost.

businessContext_i18n.en: Supplier-imposed minimum batch size. If EOQ < MOQ, order quantity must be adjusted upward, increasing holding cost.

inputs.label: Lead Time (days)
inputs.businessContext: Time from order placement to receipt. Used for safety stock calculation and reorder point.

businessContext_i18n.en: Time from order placement to receipt. Used for safety stock calculation and reorder point.

inputs.label: Demand Standard Deviation (units/day)
inputs.businessContext: Daily demand variability. Critical for safety stock under Six Sigma service levels.

businessContext_i18n.en: Daily demand variability. Critical for safety stock under Six Sigma service levels.

inputs.label: Service Level (%)
inputs.businessContext: Desired probability of not stocking out during lead time. Used to derive z-score for safety stock.

businessContext_i18n.en: Desired probability of not stocking out during lead time. Used to derive z-score for safety stock.

inputs.label: Operating Days per Year
inputs.businessContext: Number of days the facility operates. Converts annual demand to daily demand.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of days the facility operates. Converts annual demand to daily demand.

inputs.label: Storage Capacity (units)
inputs.businessContext: Maximum inventory the warehouse can physically hold. Constraint for feasible order quantity.

businessContext_i18n.en: Maximum inventory the warehouse can physically hold. Constraint for feasible order quantity.

inputs.label: Backorder Cost per Unit ($)
inputs.businessContext: Cost per unit of unmet demand (lost sales, penalty, expediting). Used in total cost trade-off.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost per unit of unmet demand (lost sales, penalty, expediting). Used in total cost trade-off.

inputs.label: Supplier Reliability Factor
inputs.businessContext: Probability that supplier delivers on time. Adjusts safety stock for lead time variability (Lean JIT).

businessContext_i18n.en: Probability that supplier delivers on time. Adjusts safety stock for lead time variability (Lean JIT).

inputs.label: Inventory Policy
inputs.businessContext: Review method: continuous (Q,R) or periodic (P,T). Affects safety stock formula.

businessContext_i18n.en: Review method: continuous (Q,R) or periodic (P,T). Affects safety stock formula.

inputs.label: Force MOQ Compliance
inputs.businessContext: If true, order quantity is rounded up to MOQ when EOQ < MOQ. If false, EOQ is used even if below MOQ (may violate supplier terms).

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, order quantity is rounded up to MOQ when EOQ < MOQ. If false, EOQ is used even if below MOQ (may violate supplier terms).

rules.message: MOQ must not exceed storage capacity. Consider negotiating lower MOQ or expanding storage.
Annual demand must be greater than zero.
Service level must be between 50% and 99.99%.
Lead time must be greater than zero days.
Order frequency is less than 2 per year. Consider reducing MOQ to avoid excessive inventory.

thresholds.message: Total inventory value exceeds 30% of annual COGS. High carrying cost risk.
Inventory turnover is below 4 (WERC benchmark). Consider reducing order quantity or increasing demand.
MOQ penalty cost exceeds 10% of total annual cost. Renegotiate MOQ or consolidate orders.

formulas.label: Daily Demand
formulas.description: Average daily demand based on annual demand and operating days.

formulas.label: Economic Order Quantity
formulas.description: Classic EOQ formula minimizing total ordering and holding costs.

formulas.label: Adjusted Order Quantity (with MOQ)
formulas.description: Order quantity after applying MOQ constraint. If MOQ > EOQ and force MOQ, use MOQ; else use EOQ.
formulas.formula: IF(use_moq_override AND moq > eoq, moq, eoq)

formulas.label: Safety Stock
formulas.description: Safety stock based on service level z-score, lead time, demand variability, and supplier reliability.

formulas.label: Reorder Point
formulas.description: Inventory level at which to place a new order (continuous review).

formulas.label: Total Annual Inventory Cost
formulas.description: Sum of ordering, holding, and backorder costs. Includes MOQ penalty if applicable.
formulas.formula: (annual_demand / adjusted_order_quantity) * ordering_cost + (adjusted_order_quantity / 2 + safety_stock) * holding_cost_per_unit + (annual_demand * backorder_cost_per_unit * (1 - service_level/100))

formulas.label: MOQ Penalty Cost
formulas.description: Extra holding cost incurred due to ordering above EOQ because of MOQ.

formulas.label: Inventory Turnover
formulas.description: Number of times inventory is sold and replaced per year (WERC metric).

primary.label: Optimal Order Quantity
primary.description: The recommended order quantity balancing MOQ, EOQ, and storage constraints.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excess Holding Cost from MOQ
hiddenLossDrivers.description: The additional holding cost incurred because MOQ forces larger order sizes than EOQ.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Demand Uncertainty Cost
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Cost of safety stock due to demand variability (Six Sigma waste).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Supplier Unreliability Impact
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Extra safety stock needed due to supplier reliability < 1.0.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Backorder Risk Cost
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Expected annual backorder cost based on service level.

suggestedActions.action: Negotiate with supplier to reduce MOQ to at least {eoq} units to save ${moq_penalty_cost} annually.
Work with supplier to improve on-time delivery above 90% to reduce safety stock by {safety_stock * (1 - supplier_reliability)} units.
Consider increasing service level to 95% to reduce backorder costs. Additional safety stock cost is ${safety_stock_increase_cost}.
Order frequency is high. Consider consolidating orders to reduce ordering costs. Target order interval: {30} days.
Storage capacity utilization exceeds 80%. Expand storage or reduce order quantity to avoid congestion.
